title: Decreased Renal Potassium Excretion
buzzwords: most common mechanism of high serum potassium
rising potassium tracks declining glomerular filtration
cortical collecting duct fails to secrete
RAAS blockade and NSAIDs unmask the problem
spironolactone, eplerenone, amiloride implicated
type IV RTA with normal-gap acidosis
GFR below 15-30 mL/min in CKD precipitates it
hypoaldosteronism impairs principal-cell K+ secretion

title: Transcellular Potassium Shift
buzzwords: intracellular store leaks into the serum
Na+/K+-ATPase suppression drives the rise
acidemia exchanges H+ in for K+ out
insulin deficiency lowers pump activity
succinylcholine, digoxin, beta-blockers implicated
hyperglycemic osmotic drag pulls K+ from cells
rapidly reversible with insulin or beta-2 agonist
DKA's classic mechanism of hyperkalemia

title: Rhabdomyolysis-Induced Hyperkalemia
buzzwords: massive muscle injury dumps intracellular contents
crush victim with dark tea-colored urine
heme-positive dipstick without red blood cells
creatine kinase soars into the thousands
compounded by AKI from pigment nephropathy
hyperphosphatemia and hypocalcemia accompany it
myoglobin tubular obstruction worsens excretion
crush-injury potassium release with peaked T waves

title: Tumor Lysis Syndrome
buzzwords: bulky malignancy lysed after starting therapy
intracellular contents flood the circulation
hyperkalemia, hyperphosphatemia, hyperuricemia together
secondary hypocalcemia from phosphate binding
acute kidney injury from urate crystal deposition
Burkitt lymphoma and acute leukemias notorious
prevent with allopurinol, rasburicase, IV hydration
chemotherapy-induced potassium release within 72 hours

title: Hyperkalemic ECG Changes
buzzwords: obtain immediately when K+ exceeds 5.5 mEq/L
progressive conduction abnormality with rising levels
shortened QT interval may appear early
PR prolongation precedes loss of P waves
QRS widens beyond 0.12 seconds
sine-wave morphology heralds asystole above 8 mEq/L
tall, thin, pointy T waves at K+ > 6.5
peaked T waves are the classic finding

title: IV Calcium Gluconate (Membrane Stabilization)
buzzwords: given emergently when ECG shows changes
does not lower the serum potassium level
onset within 2-5 minutes, duration 30-60
withhold if the ECG is normal
raises threshold potential of cardiac myocytes
chloride form preferred via central line
buys time for shifters and removal agents
calcium stabilizes the cardiac membrane in hyperkalemia

title: Insulin and Glucose (Potassium Shifter)
buzzwords: rapid temporary lowering of serum potassium
co-administered dextrose prevents hypoglycemia
onset 10-20 minutes, duration 2-6 hours
must be paired with a removal therapy
drives K+ back into the intracellular space
albuterol nebulizer shares the same mechanism
bicarbonate also shifts K+ if acidotic
stimulates Na+/K+-ATPase to internalize potassium

title: Potassium Binders and Removal
buzzwords: definitive lowering of total body potassium
loop diuretics with IV fluids enhance urinary loss
hemodialysis is the rescue in ESRD or anuria
patiromer and zirconium cyclosilicate are newer agents
binding resins exchange K+ in the GI tract
slow onset compared with shifters and calcium
risk of intestinal necrosis with older resin
sodium polystyrene sulfonate (Kayexalate)
